original_image_tags: A slightly aged, off-white envelope. The left edge has two circular punch holes. There is a blue 5-cent United States postage stamp in the upper left corner, featuring a profile portrait of Abraham Lincoln. The stamp text reads: "UNITED STATES POSTAGE" and "5 CENTS 5". A circular postmark overlaps the stamp and is centered near the top. To the right of the postmark are horizontal wavy cancellation lines. The address is handwritten in cursive ink.

elements.description: Photograph of a vintage envelope. Features one blue 5-cent United States Postage stamp in the top left corner depicting a profile portrait. A circular postmark stamp is visible to the right of the postage stamp, reading 'NEW BRUNSWICK N.J. / JUL 5 / 2 PM / 1947', followed by five wavy cancellation lines. Handwritten cursive text is centered on the envelope: 'The A.B.D. / Washington / District of Columbia'. The envelope has two circular hole-punch marks on the left edge.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual elements: a vintage envelope with a blue 5-cent stamp, circular postmark, wavy cancellation lines, handwritten address, and two punch holes on the left edge, but Description B provides specific postmark text ('NEW BRUNSWICK N.J. / JUL 5 / 2 PM / 1947') and address text ('The A.B.D. / Washington / District of Columbia') that Description A does not detail, without contradiction.
